prerequisites: A running n8n instance (self-hosted or n8n Cloud) reachable over HTTPS.
A paid n8n plan: the public API is not available during the free trial, so the account must be upgraded before an API key can be used.
The instance host/base URL for the public API, in the form https://<your-instance-url>/api/v1 (self-hosted) or https://<name>.app.n8n.cloud/api/v1 (Cloud), used for the host config field.
An n8n API key created in the n8n UI, used for the api_key config field.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: n8n authenticates the public REST API with an API key. Supply it in the connector's api_key config field; n8n expects it on requests in the X-N8N-API-KEY HTTP header. Create the key in the n8n UI under Settings > n8n API.

setupSteps.title: Confirm public API access
setupSteps.body: Make sure your n8n account is on a paid plan. The n8n public API is not available during the free trial, so upgrade first if needed. The API is available for both self-hosted and Cloud deployments.

setupSteps.title: Create an n8n API key
setupSteps.body: Log in to n8n, go to Settings > n8n API, and select Create an API key. Choose a Label and set an Expiration time for the key, then copy the generated key value. Store it securely because it grants access to your live n8n data.

setupSteps.title: Determine the host (API base URL)
setupSteps.body: Set host to your instance's public API base URL. For self-hosted instances use https://<your-instance-url>/api/v1; for n8n Cloud use https://<name>.app.n8n.cloud/api/v1. On self-hosted instances you can verify the API is enabled by opening the built-in playground at <N8N_HOST>:<N8N_PORT>/<N8N_PATH>/api/v1/docs (for example localhost:5678/api/v1/docs).

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the API key from the previous step into the api_key field and the API base URL into the host field. The connector sends the key to n8n via the X-N8N-API-KEY header on each request.

sources.title: Use an n8n API playground (docs source)
